_description: Universal Python transformation patterns — client-agnostic, applicable to any health plan roster

applicable_fields: any column with date/effective/expiration/term in name
transforms.input: Date strings in any format (M/D/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, MM-DD-YYYY, two-digit years)
transforms.business_rule: All dates standardized to ISO; years outside 1901-2099 cleared as invalid
transforms.trigger: Apply to ALL columns whose mapped_field contains 'Date', 'dob', 'expir', 'effective', or 'term' in the field name. Always needed for date columns.
transforms.when_to_skip: Non-date columns. Already normalized YYYY-MM-DD values (but safe to re-apply).

transforms.input: Identifier with possible non-digit chars, scientific notation
transforms.output: Exact N digits or empty string
transforms.business_rule: Strip non-digits, zero-pad if shorter, clear if wrong length
transforms.python_code:
def normalize_digits_exact(df, cols, exact_len):
    for col in cols:
        if col not in df.columns:
            continue
        before = df[col].astype(str).str.strip()
        before = before.where(~before.str.lower().isin(['nan','none','null','']), '')
        after = before.str.replace(r'\D', '', regex=True)
        after = after.apply(lambda x: x.zfill(exact_len) if 0 < len(x) <= exact_len else x)
        invalid = after.str.len() != exact_len
        df.loc[~invalid, col] = after[~invalid]
        df.loc[invalid & (before != ''), col] = ''
transforms.trigger: Apply when: (a) sample values contain hyphens, spaces, or parentheses in an ID column; (b) column is NPI/TIN/SSN/Phone/CAQH — normalize to digit-only with correct length.
transforms.when_to_skip: ID values already clean digit-only strings of correct length.

transforms.business_rule: Excel corrupts long numbers to scientific notation; restore to full digits
transforms.trigger: Apply when: ANY sample value in the column matches scientific notation pattern (e.g., '1.23456789E9', '4.61314303E8'). Even 1 affected value means the whole column needs this. Common in Excel exports of NPI, TIN, ZIP.
transforms.when_to_skip: No scientific notation values detected in column samples.

transforms.business_rule: Excel adds .0 to integers; strip for clean IDs
transforms.trigger: Apply when: sample values end in '.0' (e.g., '1234567890.0'). Common when Excel reads NPI/TIN as float.
transforms.when_to_skip: Values don't have trailing .0

transforms.python_code:
def normalize_time_hhmm(text: str) -> str:
    t = str(text).strip()
    if not t or t.lower() in ['', 'nan', 'none', 'nat']:
        return ''
    if t.lower() == 'closed':
        return 'Closed'
    t = t.replace('.', ':')
    t = re.sub(r'\s+', '', t)
    am = t.lower().endswith('am')
    pm = t.lower().endswith('pm')
    t = re.sub(r'(?i)(am|pm)$', '', t)
    parts = t.split(':')
    try:
        hh = int(parts[0])
        mm = int(parts[1]) if len(parts) > 1 else 0
    except (ValueError, IndexError):
        return ''
    if pm and hh < 12: hh += 12
    if am and hh == 12: hh = 0
    if 0 <= hh <= 23 and 0 <= mm <= 59:
        return f'{hh:02d}:{mm:02d}'
    return ''
transforms.trigger: Apply to openTimeMon-Sun, closeTimeMon-Sun columns — any column containing time values in non-HH:MM format.
transforms.when_to_skip: Values already in HH:MM format, or column is not a time column.

transforms.business_rule: All null equivalents standardized to empty for consistent processing
transforms.trigger: Apply to ALL columns. Always run first in the transform pipeline. Converts 'N/A', 'None', 'null', '#N/A', 'Pending', 'TBD', 'not provided', etc. to empty string.
transforms.when_to_skip: Never skip — safe to apply universally.

transforms.input: Text with tabs, newlines, multiple spaces
transforms.trigger: Apply to ALL text columns. Always run early in pipeline. Removes leading/trailing spaces, collapses internal multiple spaces.
transforms.when_to_skip: Numeric columns (but safe to apply to all).

transforms.trigger: Apply when: column is mapped to any state field (stateLicenseIssuingState, groupPracticeLocationState, etc.) AND sample values contain full state names (e.g., 'California', 'Texas') instead of 2-letter codes.
transforms.when_to_skip: Values already 2-letter uppercase state codes.

transforms.python_code: GENDER_MAP = {'male': 'M', 'female': 'F', 'unknown': 'U', 'm': 'M', 'f': 'F', 'u': 'U', 'not listed': 'U'}
transforms.trigger: Apply when: column is mapped to 'gender' field AND sample values are not already 'M'/'F'/'U' (e.g., 'Male', 'female', 'Unknown', '1', '2').
transforms.when_to_skip: Values already in M/F/U format.

transforms.trigger: Apply when: column is a boolean/Y-N field AND values are 'Yes'/'No', 'True'/'False', '1'/'0', 'YES'/'NO' instead of 'Y'/'N'.
transforms.when_to_skip: Values already 'Y'/'N'.

transforms.python_code: NEW_PTS_MAP = {'y': 'Accepting New', 'yes': 'Accepting New', 'n': 'Closed', 'no': 'Closed', 'existing': 'Existing Patients Only', 'telemedicine': 'Telemedicine', 'closed': 'Closed'}
transforms.trigger: Apply when: column is mapped to 'acceptsNewPatients' AND sample values include 'Existing Patients Only', 'Closed', 'Accepting' etc. beyond simple Y/N.
transforms.when_to_skip: Values already Y/N or standard platform values.

transforms.business_rule: Dots removed, uppercase, split on comma/slash
transforms.python_code:
def split_degrees_inline(df):
    raw = df['Degree'].astype(str).fillna('')
    parts = raw.str.replace('.', '', regex=False).str.upper().str.split(r'[,/]', expand=True)
    for i in range(5):
        col = f'Degree_{i+1}'
        df[col] = parts[i].str.strip() if i < parts.shape[1] else ''
    df.drop(columns=['Degree'], inplace=True)
transforms.trigger: Apply when: column is mapped to practitionerDegree/providerType AND sample values contain comma or slash separators (e.g., 'MD, DO', 'NP/PA'). Data profile shows multi-value separator present.
transforms.when_to_skip: Single value per row — no separators detected.

transforms.business_rule: Match against reference list using dots/hyphens/spaces stripped, case-insensitive
transforms.python_code:
def normalize_degree_values(series: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    """Normalize degree values to standard abbreviations.
    'M.D.' → 'MD', 'ph-d' → 'PHD', 'Do' → 'DO'
    """
    DEGREE_MAP = {
        "MD": ["M.D.", "M.D", "MD", "Doctor of Medicine"],
        "DO": ["D.O.", "D.O", "DO", "Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine"],
        "PHD": ["Ph.D.", "Ph.D", "PHD", "Doctor of Philosophy"],
        "DPM": ["D.P.M.", "DPM", "Doctor of Podiatric Medicine"],
        "DDS": ["D.D.S.", "DDS", "Doctor of Dental Surgery"],
        "DMD": ["D.M.D.", "DMD", "Doctor of Dental Medicine"],
        "NP": ["N.P.", "NP", "Nurse Practitioner", "NP-C", "NP-BC"],
        "PA": ["P.A.", "PA", "PA-C", "Physician Assistant"],
        "APRN": ["A.P.R.N.", "APRN", "Advanced Practice Registered Nurse"],
        "RN": ["R.N.", "RN", "Registered Nurse"],
        "LPN": ["L.P.N.", "LPN", "Licensed Practical Nurse"],
        "LCSW": ["L.C.S.W.", "LCSW", "Licensed Clinical Social Worker"],
        "LMFT": ["L.M.F.T.", "LMFT", "Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist"],
        "PSYD": ["Psy.D.", "PsyD", "PSYD", "Doctor of Psychology"],
        "OD": ["O.D.", "OD", "Doctor of Optometry"],
        "DC": ["D.C.", "DC", "Doctor of Chiropractic"],
        "MS": ["M.S.", "MS", "Master of Science"],
        "MBA": ["M.B.A.", "MBA", "Master of Business Administration"],
    }
    # Build reverse lookup: normalized_key → standard
    reverse_map = {}
    for standard, variants in DEGREE_MAP.items():
        for v in variants:
            key = re.sub(r"[.\-\s]+", "", v.upper())
            reverse_map[key] = standard

    def normalize_one(val):
        if pd.isna(val) or str(val).strip() == "":
            return ""
        cleaned = re.sub(r"[.\-\s]+", "", str(val).strip().upper())
        return reverse_map.get(cleaned, str(val).strip())

    return series.apply(normalize_one)
transforms.trigger: Apply when: column is mapped to practitionerDegree/providerType/educationDegree AND values have inconsistent formatting (dots, hyphens, full names). Run AFTER split_degrees if both needed.
transforms.when_to_skip: Values already standard abbreviations (MD, DO, NP, etc.)

applicable_fields: AddressType + Address fields → typed address columns
transforms.input: Rows with AddressType='MFC Mailing Address' and generic Address/City/State/Zip
transforms.output: Typed columns: mailingAddressLine1, billingAddressLine1, w9AddressLine1, practiceAddressLine1
transforms.business_rule: Address type masks determine which typed columns get populated
transforms.python_code:
def split_address_by_type(df: pd.DataFrame, addr_type_col: str = "AddressType",
                          addr1_col: str = "Address", city_col: str = "City",
                          state_col: str = "State", zip_col: str = "Zip") -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Split address rows by address type mask into typed columns.
    AddressType='MFC Mailing Address' → mailingAddressLine1, mailingCity, etc.
    ADDRESS_TYPE_MAP maps type keywords to column prefixes.
    """
    ADDRESS_TYPE_MAP = {
        "MAILING": "mailing",
        "BILLING": "billing",
        "PRACTICE": "practice",
        "W9": "w9",
        "W-9": "w9",
        "REMITTANCE": "remittance",
        "SERVICE": "practice",
    }
    def get_prefix(addr_type):
        if pd.isna(addr_type):
            return "practice"
        upper = str(addr_type).upper()
        for keyword, prefix in ADDRESS_TYPE_MAP.items():
            if keyword in upper:
                return prefix
        return "practice"

    df = df.copy()
    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        prefix = get_prefix(row.get(addr_type_col, ""))
        for src_col, suffix in [(addr1_col, "AddressLine1"), (city_col, "City"),
                                (state_col, "State"), (zip_col, "Zip")]:
            dest = f"{prefix}{suffix}"
            if dest not in df.columns:
                df[dest] = ""
            df.at[row.name, dest] = row.get(src_col, "")
    return df
transforms.trigger: Apply ONLY when: file has BOTH a generic address column (e.g., 'PRACTICE ADDRESS') AND a separate 'ADDRESS TYPE' column with routing values (P=practice, C=billing, M=mailing, F=facility). This is rare — seen in specific client files (Evry/Health Plan 3).
transforms.when_to_skip: File already has typed address columns (groupPracticeLocationAddressLine1, groupBillingAddress1, etc.) — system handles routing via entityInstanceGrouping automatically. DO NOT apply universally.
transforms.system_note: If file has properly named typed columns (Practice Address, Billing Address, etc.), NO transform needed — the system's entityInstanceGrouping routes them correctly.

applicable_fields: Office Hours text → openTimeMon/closeTimeMon through Sun
transforms.input: Multi-line: 'Monday: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM\nTuesday: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM'
transforms.business_rule: Parse day names, extract open/close times, normalize to HH:MM
transforms.trigger: Apply ONLY when: file has a single 'Office Hours' or 'Hours' text column containing multi-day, multi-line hours (e.g., 'Monday: 9AM-5PM; Tuesday: 9AM-5PM'). Detected by sample values showing day+time pattern.
transforms.when_to_skip: File already has separate openTimeMon, closeTimeMon etc. columns. Or hours column has simple single-line value.

transforms.input: Single row with PlanName, PlanName.1, PlanName.2 (multiple plans)
transforms.output: Multiple rows, one per plan, all other fields duplicated
transforms.business_rule: Each plan-set becomes a separate row for downstream processing
transforms.trigger: Apply ONLY when: file has NUMBERED plan columns like PlanName, PlanName.1, PlanName.2 (multiple plans encoded horizontally in one row). Detected by numbered_columns_pivot structural pattern in column headers.
transforms.when_to_skip: File has only one plan column, or plan data is already in separate rows.

transforms.input: DataFrame with rows that are >80% empty
transforms.business_rule: Sparse rows are typically formatting artifacts from Excel; 80% threshold
transforms.trigger: Apply to ALL files during initial loading. Removes rows that are >80% empty (Excel blank rows, header rows, totals rows).
transforms.when_to_skip: Never skip — always run as first cleanup step.

transforms.trigger: Apply to ALL files after initial cleanup. Removes exact duplicate rows (same NPI + same group + same location).
transforms.when_to_skip: File is known to be deduplicated already, but safe to run anyway.

transforms.trigger: Apply to ALL files before BigQuery upload. Removes special characters from column names, ensures valid BQ column naming.
transforms.when_to_skip: Never skip if uploading to BigQuery.

transforms.business_rule: Medicare IDs should not contain hyphens for downstream system processing
transforms.trigger: Apply when: column is mapped to 'medicareId' AND sample values contain hyphens (e.g., '7N-28-41').
transforms.when_to_skip: Medicare IDs already without hyphens.

transforms.output: PDM values: 'Add to Network', 'Terminate from Network', etc.
transforms.python_code:
def transaction_type_normalization(series: pd.Series,
                                   synonyms: dict = None) -> pd.Series:
    """Normalize client-supplied transaction type values to PDM canonical values.
    'New' → 'Add to Network', 'Term' → 'Terminate from Network', etc.
    Unknown values are passed through unchanged.
    """
    if synonyms is None:
        # Default mapping from reference_data.json transaction_type_synonyms
        synonyms = {
            "NEW": "Add to Network",
            "NEW LOCATION": "Add to Network",
            "NEW ROLE": "Add to Network",
            "ADD": "Add to Network",
            "ADD TO NETWORK": "Add to Network",
            "ADD TO GROUP": "Add to Group",
            "ADD TO PLAN": "Add to Plan",
            "ADD ROLE": "Add Role",
            "ADD SPECIALTY": "Add Specialty",
            "ADD LOCATION": "Add Location",
            "TERM": "Terminate from Network",
            "TERMINATE": "Terminate from Network",
            "TERMINATION": "Terminate from Network",
            "TERM FROM NETWORK": "Terminate from Network",
            "TERM FROM GROUP": "Terminate from Group",
            "TERM FROM LOCATION": "Terminate from Location",
            "TERMINATE FROM NETWORK": "Terminate from Network",
            "TERMINATE FROM GROUP": "Terminate from Group",
            "TERMINATE FROM LOCATION": "Terminate from Location",
            "UPDATE": "Update Information",
            "UPDATE INFORMATION": "Update Information",
            "CHANGE": "Update Information",
            "CORRECTION": "Update Information",
            "RELOCATION": "Terminate from Location",
        }

    def normalize_one(val):
        if pd.isna(val) or str(val).strip() == "":
            return ""
        key = str(val).strip().upper()
        return synonyms.get(key, str(val).strip())

    return series.apply(normalize_one)
transforms.trigger: Apply when: action/transaction column detected AND values are client-specific variants (e.g., 'New', 'Term', 'Relocation') rather than platform canonical values ('Add to Network', 'Terminate from Location').
transforms.when_to_skip: Values already match platform canonical transaction type names.

transforms.trigger: Apply when: license/DEA/CDS column HEADER contains a 2-letter state prefix (e.g., 'DC LICENSE NUMBER', 'MD DEA NUMBER', 'VA CDS EXPIRATION'). The state is encoded in the column name, not the data values. Detected by: header matches pattern [A-Z]{2}\s+(LICENSE|DEA|CDS|CONTROLLED SUBSTANCE) in a practitioner file.
transforms.when_to_skip: File already has separate 'License State' column alongside the license number column. Or header does not have state prefix.
applicable_fields: stateLicenseNumber (when header has state prefix like 'DC LICENSE NUMBER')
practitionerDea (when header has state prefix like 'DC DEA NUMBER')
practitionerControlledSubstanceNumber (when header has state prefix like 'DC CDS NUMBER')
transforms.input: Column header 'DC LICENSE NUMBER' with values ['V0226', 'M1234', ...]
transforms.output: Maps column to stateLicenseNumber. ALSO creates derived column stateLicenseIssuingState filled with 'DC' for all rows.
transforms.business_rule: The system's objectArrayGrouping for licenses requires BOTH stateLicenseNumber AND stateLicenseIssuingState to create a complete license record. When the state is in the header, the AI must extract it and populate stateLicenseIssuingState. Without this, license state = null in the system.
transforms.system_requirement: REQUIRED — without stateLicenseIssuingState, the license array object has state=null. System stores incomplete license data. Cannot do state-board validation.
examples: DC LICENSE NUMBER → stateLicenseNumber + derive stateLicenseIssuingState='DC'
MD DEA NUMBER → practitionerDea + derive practitionerDeaState='MD'
TX LICENSE STATE → stateLicenseIssuingState (this is an explicit state column, no extraction needed)
LICENSE NUMBER 1 + LICENSE STATE 1 → stateLicenseNumber + stateLicenseIssuingState (separate columns, no extraction needed)
transforms.python_code:
def state_prefix_extraction(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Detect columns with state prefix (e.g., 'DC LICENSE NUMBER') and derive state columns.

    For each column header matching [STATE][SPACE][LICENSE|DEA|CDS]:
    - Renames column to the appropriate schema field name (stateLicenseNumber, etc.)
    - Creates a new column with stateLicenseIssuingState / practitionerDeaState / practitionerControlledSubstanceState
      filled with the detected state abbreviation for all rows.

    The system's objectArrayGrouping requires both number AND state fields to create
    complete license/DEA/CDS array objects.
    """
    import re

    STATE_PATTERN = re.compile(
        r"^([A-Z]{2})\s+(LICENSE|DEA|CDS|CONTROLLED\s+SUBSTANCE)\s*(NUMBER|NUM|#|EXPIR\w*|EXP|EXEMPT|WAIVER)?",
        re.I
    )
    LICENSE_FIELD_MAP = {
        "LICENSE": {None: "stateLicenseNumber", "expir": "stateLicenseExpirationDate",
                    "exp": "stateLicenseExpirationDate", "type": "stateLicenseType"},
        "DEA": {None: "practitionerDea", "expir": "practitionerDeaExpirationDate",
                "exp": "practitionerDeaExpirationDate", "exempt": "practitionerDeaExempt",
                "waiver": "practitionerDeaWaiver"},
        "CDS": {None: "practitionerControlledSubstanceNumber",
                "expir": "practitionerControlledSubstanceExpirationDate",
                "exp": "practitionerControlledSubstanceExpirationDate",
                "exempt": "practitionerControlledSubstanceNumberExempt",
                "waiver": "practitionerControlledSubstanceNumberWaiver"},
    }
    STATE_FIELD_MAP = {
        "LICENSE": "stateLicenseIssuingState",
        "DEA": "practitionerDeaState",
        "CDS": "practitionerControlledSubstanceState",
    }

    df = df.copy()
    derived_state_cols = {}  # state_field_name → state_value (for columns we'll add)

    for col in list(df.columns):
        m = STATE_PATTERN.match(col.strip())
        if not m:
            continue

        state = m.group(1).upper()
        cred_type = m.group(2).upper().replace(" ", "")
        suffix = (m.group(3) or "").lower()[:5]

        # Normalize cred_type
        if "CONTROLLED" in cred_type or cred_type == "CDS":
            cred_type = "CDS"
        elif cred_type == "DEA":
            cred_type = "DEA"
        else:
            cred_type = "LICENSE"

        # Find target schema field
        field_map = LICENSE_FIELD_MAP.get(cred_type, {})
        target_field = None
        for key, field in field_map.items():
            if key is None and not suffix:
                target_field = field
            elif key and suffix and key in suffix:
                target_field = field
        if not target_field:
            target_field = field_map.get(None, col)

        # Rename column to target field
        if col != target_field and target_field not in df.columns:
            df.rename(columns={col: target_field}, inplace=True)

        # Record that we need to derive the state column
        state_field = STATE_FIELD_MAP.get(cred_type, "stateLicenseIssuingState")
        if state_field not in df.columns:
            derived_state_cols[state_field] = state

    # Add derived state columns (fill all rows with the extracted state)
    for state_field, state_value in derived_state_cols.items():
        df[state_field] = state_value

    return df

transforms.trigger: Apply ONLY when: file has MANY 2-letter state abbreviations as column HEADERS (e.g., AL, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DC...) with license numbers or boolean values in the cells. This is the state license matrix pattern where a practitioner has one row and licenses for multiple states encoded as separate columns. Detected by: 5+ consecutive column headers that are valid 2-letter US state abbreviations.
transforms.when_to_skip: File does not have state codes as column headers. File already has stateLicenseNumber + stateLicenseIssuingState as separate columns. Or state codes appear only in data values (not headers).
applicable_fields: stateLicenseNumber (cell value becomes the license number)
stateLicenseIssuingState (column header becomes the issuing state)
transforms.input: Wide format: columns [NPI, Name, AL, AZ, CA, TX, ...] where AL/AZ/CA/TX contain license numbers
transforms.output: Long format: multiple rows per practitioner, one per state license. Each row has stateLicenseNumber (the value) + stateLicenseIssuingState (the column header state). Empty/null cells are dropped.
transforms.business_rule: The system objectArrayGrouping for licenses requires (stateLicenseNumber + stateLicenseIssuingState) pairs. When licenses are in a wide matrix, we must melt to get one pair per row. Without melt, the system sees column headers as field names (not state codes) and cannot build the license array.
transforms.system_requirement: Required when state matrix pattern detected. Without melt, licenses are lost.
examples: Wide: NPI=1234567890, AL=A1234, AZ=None, CA=C5678 -> Long: row1(stateLicenseNumber=A1234, stateLicenseIssuingState=AL), row2(stateLicenseNumber=C5678, stateLicenseIssuingState=CA)
Empty/null cells are dropped -- only states where the practitioner has a license are kept
transforms.python_code:
def state_license_matrix_melt(df: pd.DataFrame, state_cols: list = None) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Melt state license matrix (wide) into long format.

    Detects 2-letter state code column headers and melts them into rows.
    Each non-null cell becomes a stateLicenseNumber row with stateLicenseIssuingState.
    """
    US_STATES = {
        "AL","AK","AZ","AR","CA","CO","CT","DE","FL","GA","HI","ID","IL","IN","IA",
        "KS","KY","LA","ME","MD","MA","MI","MN","MS","MO","MT","NE","NV","NH","NJ",
        "NM","NY","NC","ND","OH","OK","OR","PA","RI","SC","SD","TN","TX","UT","VT",
        "VA","WA","WV","WI","WY","DC","PR","VI","GU","AS","MP"
    }

    if state_cols is None:
        state_cols = [c for c in df.columns if str(c).strip().upper() in US_STATES]

    if not state_cols:
        return df  # No state matrix columns found

    id_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in state_cols]

    melted = df.melt(
        id_vars=id_cols,
        value_vars=state_cols,
        var_name="stateLicenseIssuingState",
        value_name="stateLicenseNumber"
    )

    # Drop rows where the practitioner has no license in that state
    melted = melted.dropna(subset=["stateLicenseNumber"])
    melted = melted[melted["stateLicenseNumber"].astype(str).str.strip() != ""]
    melted["stateLicenseIssuingState"] = melted["stateLicenseIssuingState"].str.upper()

    return melted.reset_index(drop=True)

_transform_scopes.universal: Run on ALL files as part of standard pipeline. Always safe to apply.
_transform_scopes.conditional: Run ONLY when the specific trigger condition is detected in data profile.
_transform_scopes.structural: Run ONLY when a specific file structure pattern is detected. Client-specific patterns exist here.
_transform_scopes.client_specific: Do NOT include in universal training — depends on specific client file format.
